description_long: This helicopter features a sleek, modern design with a streamlined fuselage painted in a vibrant orange and black color scheme. The main rotor consists of four long, slender blades with red tips, while the tail rotor is mounted at the end of a narrow tail boom and is partially enclosed by a protective structure. The cockpit is equipped with large, wraparound windows that provide excellent visibility for the pilot and co-pilot, framed in black and slightly tinted. Sturdy black landing skids run parallel beneath the fuselage, connected by crossbars for added stability. The helicopter also has side-mounted equipment pods and visible exhaust outlets, suggesting a utility or rescue role. No visible registration number or alphanumeric markings are present on the exterior.
description: A modern orange and black helicopter with four-blade main rotor, wraparound cockpit windows, and sturdy black landing skids. The design is sleek and functional, with side equipment pods and a partially enclosed tail rotor.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, main and tail rotors, side equipment pods, and the detailed landing skids.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide cockpit windows, rotor hub, and the symmetry of the landing skids.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other side of the fuselage and equipment pods.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the landing skids and equipment pods.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, narrow main rotor blades, slightly tapered, with black bodies and red tips. The blades appear to be made of composite material and span nearly the full width of the helicopter.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor is present at the end of the tail boom, with two visible blades oriented vertically and partially enclosed by a protective ring. The blades are black with red tips.
attribute_landing_skids: Landing skids are black, tubular, and run parallel beneath the fuselage, connected by crossbars for stability. They appear to be made of metal with a matte finish.
attribute_cockpit_windows: The cockpit features large, wraparound windows with a slightly angular shape, framed in black. The windows are tinted and provide wide visibility for the crew.
